# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create a CloudFormation template for EC2 instances with EBS volumes for our application data. Focus on getting the instances running quickly. We'll add encryption and backup policies in phase 2 after we validate the architecture.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: QuickStart for EC2 Instances with EBS Volumes

Resources:
  EC2Instance:
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::Instance'
    Properties:
      ImageId: ami-abcd1234
      InstanceType: t2.micro
      KeyName: MyKey
      IamInstanceProfile: !Ref MyEc2InstanceRole
      SecurityGroupIds:
        - !Ref MySecurityGroup

  EBSVolume:
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::Volume'
    Properties:
      AvailabilityZone: !GetAtt "EC2Instance.AvailabilityZone"
      Size: 30

  EBSAttachment:
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::VolumeAttachment'
    DependsOn: EC2Instance
    Properties:
      InstanceId: !Ref EC2Instance
      VolumeId: !Ref EBSVolume
      Device: /dev/sdh

  MyEc2InstanceRole:
    Type: 'AWS::IAM::Role'
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service:
                - ec2.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - sts:AssumeRole

  MyEc2InstanceProfile:
    Type: 'AWS::IAM::InstanceProfile'
    Properties:
      Roles:
        - !Ref MyEc2InstanceRole

  MySecurityGroup:
    Type: 'AWS::EC2::SecurityGroup'
    Properties:
      GroupDescription: Allow inbound traffic on port 22
      VpcId: vpc-abcd1234
      SecurityGroupIngress:
        - IpProtocol: tcp
          FromPort: '22'
          ToPort: '22'
          CidrIp: 0.0.0.0/0

Outputs:
  Ec2InstancePublicDnsName:
    Value: !GetAtt "EC2Instance.PublicDnsName"
    Description: Public DNS name of the EC2 instance
